description: Algorithm-robustness of operational-bridge DETECTION: run the canonical detection pipeline (label_clusters -> find_bridge_theorems -> indegree elbow/filter) UNCHANGED on partitions from 5 different community-detection algorithms (leiden_modularity baseline, louvain, fastgreedy, label_propagation, infomap) over the SAME deterministically-sorted LCC. Tests whether the scarce operational-bridge phenomenon (q-0006/q-0007) is algorithm-agnostic or a Leiden artifact -- a new robustness axis beyond seed (e-0050/e-0051) and config-null (e-0049).
motivation: Every prior robustness check varied only the Leiden random SEED or used a degree-preserving null; none tested a genuinely DIFFERENT clustering objective/optimizer. The bridge DEFINITION (premises spanning >=2 PURE clusters) inherits the partition's granularity, so algorithm choice could change scale and identity.
formal_results: For each algo A: partition P_A over the sorted LCC (n=92,594-ish, simplified igraph); pure clusters (dom module >=50%); raw operational bridges B_A = theorems with premises in >=2 distinct pure clusters; strict via in-degree elbow theta. Report n_clusters, n_pure, |B_A| raw/strict, pairwise Jaccard of bridge THEOREM sets, cross-algo core = intersection over all 5, recovery of canonical stored 280 raw / 34 strict.
results_description: n_clusters varies 138(leiden)/160(louvain)/1106(fastgreedy)/809(labelprop)/4193(infomap); n_pure 112/134/1032/774/3065. RAW bridge count: leiden 295, louvain 356, fastgreedy 98, label_prop 618, infomap 23134 (mean 4900, range 98-23134 vs canonical 280). STRICT: 44/17/66/58/4059. Pairwise Jaccard raw mean 0.029 (range 0.004-0.184), strict mean 0.018. vs-Leiden-baseline Jaccard raw: louvain 0.184 (closest), fastgreedy 0.010, label_prop 0.014, infomap 0.010. Cross-algo core (raw bridge in ALL 5): 0 theorems; only 10 in 4 algos, 92 in 3, 852 in 2, 22481 in exactly 1 (union 23435). Canonical-raw recovery: infomap 0.825, leiden 0.50, louvain 0.257, others <0.08.
conclusions: The operational-bridge population is NOT algorithm-invariant in scale or identity -- both are governed by the partition's cluster granularity/shape. The scarce ~280-raw object is specific to the modularity-resolution regime (~140-160 clusters): only Leiden and Louvain (the two modularity methods) agree at all (Jaccard 0.18, both ~300-356 raw). Finer-resolution methods reframe up to ~25% of the graph as bridges (infomap, 23k). The all-algorithm core is empty -- weaker than the seed-stable 27-core (e-0051). q-0006/q-0007 hold only RELATIVE to a fixed modularity resolution; there is no algorithm-free operational bridge. Does NOT contradict e-0049's RELATIVE depletion-vs-null claim (not re-measured per algo).
